DELETE /accounts/{account_id}/containers.description: Stops the backing Durable Object container by sending SIGKILL. The instance remains visible until normal runtime lifecycle processing marks it asleep and eventually prunes it.

schema.description: An Application ID represents an identifier of an application
A container instance ID (64-character hex Durable Object actor ID).

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

200.description: Container instance successfully stopped, or was already asleep.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

400.description: Container instance APIs are not enabled for this application or the request was invalid.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.
Whether the API call was successful.
Whether the API call was successful.

GET /accounts/{account_id}/containers/ap.description: Lists container instances for an application, including control-plane details and any associated deployment/placement information.

schema.description: An Application ID represents an identifier of an application

parameters.description: Maximum number of instances to return per page. Defaults to all.
parameters.name: per_page

parameters.description: Opaque token from a previous response to retrieve the next page.
parameters.name: page_token

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

next_page_token.description: The token to use to retrieve the next page of results.

page_token.description: The page token sent in the request.

per_page.description: The number of items per page requested.

result.description: Response body for listing container instances.

items.description: Represents a container instance within an application, backed by a Durable Object.

application_id.description: An Application ID represents an identifier of an application

created_at.description: UTC timestamp string in ISO 8601 format

deployment_id.description: A deployment ID represents an identifier of a deployment configuration that maintains a healthy placement

id.description: A container instance ID (64-character hex Durable Object actor ID).

ingress_url.description: Public ingress URL for the instance. Omitted when managed application ingress is not configured.

name.description: The human-readable name of the instance.

status.description: The current status of a container instance.

exit_code.description: The exit code of the container, if status is stopped_with_code.

last_change.description: Epoch timestamp (milliseconds) of the last status change.

status.description: The current lifecycle status of the container.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

400.description: Container instance APIs are not enabled for this application or the request was invalid.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.
Whether the API call was successful.

404.description: Response body when an Application is not found

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

POST /accounts/{account_id}/containers/a.description: Creates a new container instance within an application. The instance's container is started immediately by invoking startAndWaitForPorts on the backing Durable Object.

schema.description: An Application ID represents an identifier of an application
Request body for creating a new container instance within an application.

enable_internet.description: Whether to enable outbound internet access for the container.

entrypoint.description: Command to run in the container. Overrides the image's default entrypoint.

environment_variables.description: Environment variables to pass to the container.

name.description: A human-readable name for the instance. Used as the Durable Object name via getByName().

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

result.description: Represents a container instance within an application, backed by a Durable Object.

application_id.description: An Application ID represents an identifier of an application

created_at.description: UTC timestamp string in ISO 8601 format

deployment_id.description: A deployment ID represents an identifier of a deployment configuration that maintains a healthy placement

id.description: A container instance ID (64-character hex Durable Object actor ID).

ingress_url.description: Public ingress URL for the instance. Omitted when managed application ingress is not configured.

name.description: The human-readable name of the instance.

status.description: The current status of a container instance.

exit_code.description: The exit code of the container, if status is stopped_with_code.

last_change.description: Epoch timestamp (milliseconds) of the last status change.

status.description: The current lifecycle status of the container.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

400.description: Container instance APIs are not enabled for this application or the request was invalid.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.
Whether the API call was successful.

404.description: Response body when an Application is not found

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

POST /accounts/{account_id}/containers/a.description: Executes a command in a running container instance and returns its buffered standard output, standard error, and exit code.

schema.description: An Application ID represents an identifier of an application
A container instance ID (64-character hex Durable Object actor ID).
Request body for executing a command in a running container instance.

command.description: Command and arguments to execute without shell interpretation.

environment_variables.description: Environment variables to set for the command.

stdin.description: Base64-encoded bytes to provide to the command on stdin.

user.description: Container user to execute the command as.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

result.description: Result of executing a command in a container instance.

exit_code.description: Exit code returned by the command.

stderr.description: Base64-encoded standard error from the command.

stdout.description: Base64-encoded standard output from the command.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

400.description: Container instance APIs are not enabled for this application or the request was invalid.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.
Whether the API call was successful.
Whether the API call was successful.
Whether the API call was successful.

503.description: The container instance is not running.

POST /accounts/{account_id}/containers/a.summary: Execute a command in a container instance

POST /accounts/{account_id}/containers/a.description: Forwards an HTTP request to a running container instance by invoking fetch on the backing Durable Object. The container must be running. The container response status, headers, and text body are returned in the API response body.

schema.description: An Application ID represents an identifier of an application
A container instance ID (64-character hex Durable Object actor ID).
Request body for proxying a request to a container instance.

body.description: Request body to forward to the container.

headers.description: HTTP headers to forward to the container.

method.description: HTTP method to use. Defaults to GET.

url.description: The path to forward to the container (e.g. "/api/data").

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

result.description: Response returned by the container instance.

body.description: Text response body returned by the container.

headers.description: HTTP response headers returned by the container.

status.description: HTTP status code returned by the container.

200.description: Response returned by the container instance.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

400.description: Container instance APIs are not enabled for this application or the request was invalid.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.
Whether the API call was successful.
Whether the API call was successful.

POST /accounts/{account_id}/containers/a.summary: Proxy a request to a container instance
